options.content: Select **Lookback** above. The four cards count unique privacy-safe issue groups from existing derived snapshots. The default is **6h**, matching every focused dashboard. A zero or empty card means only that no matching derived issue snapshot exists in the selected lookback; it does not prove health.

panels.title: Stuck / incomplete runs
panels.description: Unique run_hash values with non-completed run-health evidence in the selected lookback.

panels.title: Tool issue groups
panels.description: Unique run_hash + tool_name groups with failed, missing, or unknown result evidence in the selected lookback; not individual-call proof.

targets.expr: count(sum by (run_hash, tool_name) (count_over_time({service_name="Codex Tool Diagnosis"} | event_name="codex.tool_diagnostic" | state=~"FAILED_RESULT|SELECTED_NO_RESULT|UNKNOWN_RESULT" [${lookback}])))

panels.title: API reliability groups
panels.description: Unique run_hash + endpoint_hash groups with failed, retried, slow, or incomplete evidence in the selected lookback; not individual-request proof.

targets.expr: count(sum by (run_hash, endpoint_hash) (count_over_time({service_name="Codex API Diagnosis"} | event_name="codex.api_diagnostic" | state=~"FAILED_REQUEST|RETRIED_REQUEST|SLOW_REQUEST|UNKNOWN_REQUEST" [${lookback}])))

panels.title: Slow contributor groups
panels.description: Unique shipped contributor groups in the selected lookback; contributor-level evidence, not full-turn latency.

targets.expr: count(sum by (run_hash, contributor_type, endpoint_hash, tool_name) (count_over_time({service_name="Codex Slow Contributor Diagnosis"} | event_name="codex.slow_contributor" | state=~"SLOW_API_CONTRIBUTOR|SLOW_TOOL_CONTRIBUTOR|MULTIPLE_SLOW_CONTRIBUTORS" [${lookback}])))

options.content:
| Evidence category | Open first | What to inspect |
|---|---|---|
| Stuck or incomplete run | [Codex Stuck Triage](/d/codex-stuck-burn-triage/codex-stuck-burn-triage?from=now-${lookback}&to=now) | Privacy-safe run grouping, state, quiet time, and source window |
| Failed or missing tool result | [Codex Tool Failure Diagnosis](/d/codex-tool-failure-diagnosis/codex-tool-failure-diagnosis?from=now-${lookback}&to=now) | Run/tool grouping and result evidence |
| Failed, retried, slow, or incomplete API evidence | [Codex API Request Reliability](/d/codex-api-request-reliability/codex-api-request-reliability?from=now-${lookback}&to=now) | Run/endpoint grouping, state, status bucket, and bounded duration/attempt evidence |
| Slow API or tool contributor | [Codex Slow Contributor Triage](/d/codex-slow-contributor-triage/codex-slow-contributor-triage?from=now-${lookback}&to=now) | Confirmed contributor grouping, duration, threshold, and source window |

Open the focused dashboard with the strongest non-zero category. Treat every category as investigation evidence, not proof of a product bug.

- Summarizes existing privacy-safe **derived records only**; it does not classify source telemetry directly.
- Analyzers and optional watchers must emit fresh derived snapshots. Silence or zero counts do not prove health.
- CI validates static structure and unit behavior; it does not run the local telemetry-to-Grafana path.
- These are navigation cues and investigation evidence, not proof of a product bug.
- This is a light front door for an already-running local kit, not onboarding, alerting, notification replacement, or production monitoring.
- Phase 6 stuck-notification semantics are unchanged.
